note: AI-generated from training data. Requires verification against UNFCCC and bilateral agreement sources before public launch.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.notes: Most advanced Article 6.2 framework in Africa. ITMO eligibility criteria for ARR published April 2026. Bilateral agreement with Switzerland operational. Carbon tax creates domestic demand signal.

countries.name: Ghana
countries.notes: ITMO agreement with Switzerland operational since 2023. Ghana Carbon Market Office established. Domestic carbon market framework in place. Strong institutional capacity.

countries.name: Kenya
countries.notes: Carbon markets act passed 2023. Bilateral agreements with Switzerland and Singapore. Article 6.2 ITMO framework operational. Africa Carbon Markets Initiative founding member.

countries.name: Rwanda
countries.notes: Bilateral agreement with Switzerland signed. Strong government capacity. Clean development aligned with Vision 2050. ITMO transfer protocol in place.

countries.name: Senegal
countries.notes: Bilateral agreement signed with Japan under JCM framework. ITMO eligibility criteria under development. Carbon registry being established.

countries.name: Morocco
countries.notes: Agreements with multiple bilateral partners. Renewable energy ITMO pathway well-developed. Nature-based solution framework lagging energy sector.

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.notes: Bilateral agreement signed with Switzerland. Framework development ongoing. Tigray conflict aftermath creates implementation risk in northern regions. Strong NDC ambition.

countries.name: Tanzania
countries.notes: Bilateral framework signed. Tanzania Carbon Market Platform established. MRV capacity being built. Blue carbon pathway under development for Zanzibar.

countries.name: Zambia
countries.notes: Bilateral agreement with Sweden signed. Forest carbon focus. Zambia Carbon Market Framework under development. Currency instability creates transaction risk.

countries.name: Zimbabwe
countries.notes: Government mandated 50% revenue share from carbon projects including Kariba. Bilateral agreements in progress. Political and currency risk remains significant. New carbon regulations creating project uncertainty.

countries.name: Mozambique
countries.notes: Negotiations with Norway ongoing under REDD+ framework. Northern insurgency in Cabo Delgado creates implementation risk. Strong forestry carbon potential. REDD+ programme well-established.

countries.name: Gabon
countries.notes: One of Africa's most advanced blue carbon frameworks. Gabon Blue Carbon sovereign ITMO programme operational. Post-coup political transition creates some uncertainty but carbon programme protected.

countries.name: Ivory Coast
countries.notes: JCM negotiations with Japan ongoing. Carbon market framework under development. Cocoa supply chain decarbonisation creating strong private sector demand. Article 6.4 also being explored.

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.notes: National carbon market framework established but bilateral ITMO agreements not yet signed. Regulatory capacity weak. Oil sector dominates policy attention. Large potential market.

countries.name: Cameroon
countries.notes: Bilateral negotiations ongoing. Anglophone conflict in Northwest and Southwest regions creates significant implementation risk. Strong forest carbon potential in east.

countries.name: DR Congo
countries.notes: Enormous carbon potential. Government increasingly engaged with carbon markets. Eastern conflict remains major risk. CAFI partnership providing framework. Congo Basin forest essential to global climate.

countries.name: Republic of Congo
countries.notes: CAFI partner country. Bilateral negotiations in progress. Forest carbon framework developing. Oil revenue dependence creates transition complexity.

countries.name: Cameroon
countries.notes: Bilateral negotiations ongoing with multiple parties. Anglophone conflict in SW and NW regions creates risk. Strong forest potential in east and south.

countries.name: Madagascar
countries.notes: Negotiations with Switzerland ongoing. Strong voluntary market track record through Makira and Ankeniheny projects. Government capacity limited. Political volatility a recurring concern.

countries.name: Uganda
countries.notes: National carbon framework exists. No bilateral ITMO agreements signed. East African regional coordination ongoing. Strong project pipeline but sovereign framework lagging.

countries.name: Namibia
countries.notes: Negotiations with Germany in progress. Communal conservancy model well-suited to ITMO. Strong governance institutions. Green hydrogen ambitions also driving carbon market interest.

countries.name: Botswana
countries.notes: Framework under development. Diamond revenue makes carbon less urgent. Strong governance. Okavango ITMO potential recognised but no bilateral agreement yet.

countries.name: Malawi
countries.notes: Framework in early stages. Limited institutional capacity. Strong project pipeline in north. Donor-dependent economy creates alignment opportunities.

countries.name: Sierra Leone
countries.notes: Carbon market framework announced. Gola project provides practical foundation. Limited regulatory capacity. Strong civil society engagement.

countries.name: Liberia
countries.notes: Carbon regulatory framework under development. Strong forestry potential. Norway partnership under REDD+ providing technical support. Institutional capacity building ongoing.

countries.name: Guinea
countries.notes: Military government since 2021. Carbon market engagement minimal. Strong forest potential in Ziama and Fouta Djallon. Political transition needed before meaningful framework development.

countries.name: Mali
countries.notes: Military government. Sahel conflict severely limits implementation capacity. FMNR carbon projects viable in principle but sovereign framework impossible in current context.

countries.name: Burkina Faso
countries.notes: Military government since 2022. Jihadist insurgency across much of the country. Tond Tenga project predates current crisis. No meaningful policy framework possible currently.

countries.name: Niger
countries.notes: Military government since 2023. ECOWAS sanctions. Carbon framework development suspended. FMNR potential significant but sovereign context prohibitive.

countries.name: Chad
countries.notes: Transitional military government. Chronic instability. Lake Chad basin carbon potential recognised internationally but implementation impossible in current context.

countries.name: Central African Republic
countries.notes: Ongoing armed conflict. Russian Wagner group presence complicates international engagement. Dzanga-Sangha project isolated from national framework. Enormous forest carbon potential unreachable.

countries.name: South Sudan
countries.notes: Fragile peace. Sudd wetland represents one of Africa's largest untapped carbon opportunities. No sovereign framework possible until political stability achieved.

countries.name: Somalia
countries.notes: Federal government fragile. Al-Shabaab controls significant territory. Carbon framework development not viable. Puntland and Somaliland show more potential as sub-national actors.

countries.name: Libya
countries.notes: Divided government. No unified authority to negotiate or implement bilateral agreements. Oil state with minimal carbon market interest.

countries.name: Sudan
countries.notes: Civil war since April 2023. Humanitarian catastrophe. No policy framework possible. Gum arabic belt carbon potential remains theoretical until conflict resolved.

countries.name: Tunisia
countries.notes: Carbon market framework in early stages. Presidential consolidation of power creates governance uncertainty. EU adjacency drives some alignment. Green belt project in development.

countries.name: Algeria
countries.notes: Hydrocarbon-dependent state. Limited engagement with international carbon markets. Great Green Wall nominal participation. Bilateral ITMO not on policy agenda.

countries.name: Egypt
countries.notes: COP27 host built political capital but domestic framework weak. Voluntary carbon market activity growing. Nature-based solutions limited by geography. Renewable energy ITMO more viable.

countries.name: Eritrea
countries.notes: Isolated authoritarian state. No international carbon market engagement. Dahlak mangrove potential unreachable in current context.

countries.name: Djibouti
countries.notes: Small state. Strategic port location dominates policy. Limited carbon market engagement. Mangrove and marine potential recognised but no framework.

countries.name: Seychelles
countries.notes: Blue bond pioneer. Article 6.2 framework operational for marine and coastal carbon. Small land area but significant blue carbon potential. Strong international partnerships.

countries.name: Mauritius
countries.notes: Framework under development. Small island state. Blue carbon focus. Strong institutions but limited forest carbon opportunity.

countries.name: Comoros
countries.notes: Political instability. Limited institutional capacity. Forest and blue carbon potential on Anjouan. No bilateral framework.

countries.name: Sao Tome and Principe
countries.notes: Small island state. Framework in early stages. Obô rainforest carbon potential significant relative to country size. Strong governance but limited capacity.

countries.name: Cabo Verde
countries.notes: Strong governance. Limited land carbon opportunity. Blue carbon and ocean-based solutions more viable. No formal ITMO framework yet despite good institutional base.

countries.name: Guinea-Bissau
countries.notes: Chronic political instability. Bijagos blue carbon significant. Limited institutional capacity to develop sovereign framework.

countries.name: Gambia
countries.notes: Small state. Post-Jammeh recovery continues. Framework under development. Mangrove carbon most viable pathway given geography.

countries.name: Togo
countries.notes: Carbon market framework announced. Limited forest base. Fazao-Malfakassa project viable. Institutional capacity developing.

countries.name: Benin
countries.notes: Negotiations with Switzerland progressing. Pendjari biosphere strong candidate for ITMO. Good governance improving. W-Arly-Pendjari transboundary complex creates regional opportunity.

countries.name: Equatorial Guinea
countries.notes: Oil-dependent autocracy. Limited carbon market engagement despite significant forest cover. Monte Alen project isolated from national policy.

countries.name: Angola
countries.notes: Oil transition creating carbon market interest. Framework in early stages. Okavango headwaters carbon significant. Institutional capacity building underway.

countries.name: Eswatini
countries.notes: Absolute monarchy. Limited carbon market engagement. Lubombo conservancy project in development. No bilateral ITMO framework.

countries.name: Lesotho
countries.notes: Highland watershed carbon potential. Limited institutional capacity. Political fragility a recurring concern. No bilateral framework.

countries.name: Mauritania
countries.notes: Great Green Wall participant. Limited carbon market engagement. Sahel reforestation potential. No bilateral ITMO framework despite NDC ambition.
